title: Data Efficient Adaptation in Large Language Models via Continuous Low-Rank Fine-Tuning

repos.name: Applied-Machine-Learning-Lab__DEAL
repos.evidence: README states 'Our paper has been accepted by NeurIPS 2025' and title exactly matches the paper. Paper text (line 46) gives official URL https://github.com/Applied-Machine-Learning-Lab/DEAL. Repo contains src/T5_run_wavelet.py, src/Llama3_run_wavelet.py, src/waveletLoRAAdapter.py implementing the DEAL wavelet-LoRA method, CL_Benchmark/ with the 15-task data, and even the paper PDF paper/2025_NIPS_HanXiaoZimo_ContineFTLora.pdf. Repo owner 'Applied-Machine-Learning-Lab' corresponds to the paper's co-author lab (Xiangyu Zhao, City University of Hong Kong).

rationale: The single cloned repo Applied-Machine-Learning-Lab__DEAL is the paper's own official implementation. The README explicitly announces NeurIPS 2025 acceptance and matches the paper title verbatim. Core method files (waveletLoRAAdapter.py, T5_run_wavelet.py, Llama3_run_wavelet.py) implement the DEAL wavelet-LoRA continual fine-tuning framework described in the paper. All audit findings are correctly targeted at this repo: high-severity bugs (reg-loss-never-applied, long-t5-script-crashes), difference findings (wavelet-method-differs-from-paper, adapter-update-both-vs-A-only), and missing findings (baselines-not-in-repo, stat-test-not-implemented) are genuine reproducibility issues with this paper's own code. No non-core repos were cloned.
audit_impact: None. The audit correctly identified the paper's own implementation and all findings are validly written against it. No misclassification or contamination detected.
recommended_action: No change needed. The paper is correctly classified as source-present and all findings stand.
